title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: April 25, 2026 - Tech-Led Rally Continues Amid Geopolitical Optimism

items.headline: U.S. Equities Rally on Tech Strength and Hopes for Mideast De-escalation
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed Friday on a high note, extending a 'risk-on' posture seen throughout April. The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 both reached new all-time highs, driven largely by robust performance in the technology and semiconductor sectors. Investor sentiment was further boosted by optimism surrounding potential US-Iran peace talks and clarity on Federal Reserve leadership. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, lagged, showing a bifurcation in market performance. Global tensions, particularly in the Middle East, continue to influence commodity markets, though investors appear to be focusing on long-term economic fundamentals. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Intel Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Intel ($INTC) shares surged approximately 23% to an all-time high after reporting a stunning first-quarter earnings beat and providing a positive forecast. Foundry revenue climbed 16% year-over-year, and data center and AI revenue rose 22% amid increasing demand for its CPUs.

items.headline: AMD Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) saw its stock price soar 14% today, benefiting from the broader semiconductor rally and strong demand in the tech sector.

items.headline: Infosys Plunges on Muted Outlook
items.summary: Infosys ($INFY) shares plunged seven percent on Friday, closing at Rs 1,154.80. The decline was triggered by disappointing earnings and a cautious muted revenue outlook, which impacted the entire IT sector.

items.headline: HCA Healthcare Falls Short on Earnings
items.summary: HCA Healthcare ($HCA) experienced an 8.76% decline in its stock price after its latest earnings report fell short of analyst expectations, leading the healthcare sector lower.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductor Sectors Lead Market Gains
items.summary: Information Technology was the top-performing sector, gaining 2.46% on Friday, with the semiconductor sector extending its record-breaking streak to 18 consecutive days of gains. Companies like Intel and AMD led the charge, reflecting continued strong demand and investor confidence in tech and AI-related growth.

items.headline: Healthcare Sector Lags Amid Earnings Misses
items.summary: The healthcare sector was the worst-performing on Friday, down 1.37%. This was largely due to several large-cap names, including HCA Healthcare, posting disappointing earnings reports that fell short of expectations.

items.headline: New Fed Chair Appointment Boosts Market Confidence
items.summary: Optimism surrounding the future of monetary policy increased as Kevin Warsh was cleared to take the helm of the U.S. central bank as the new Federal Reserve Chair. This development, along with the Trump administration's decision to suspend legal investigations, has bolstered expectations for a more accommodative policy outlook.

items.headline: United Bankshares Reports Strong Q1 Earnings
items.summary: United Bankshares Inc. ($UBSI) announced first-quarter 2026 earnings of $124.2 million, or $0.89 per diluted share. The company highlighted strong earnings, sound asset quality, and efficient capital allocation despite geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainties.

items.headline: Dow Inc. Reports Q1 Net Loss, Cites Middle East Conflict Impact
items.summary: Dow Inc. ($DOW) reported a GAAP net loss of $445 million for the first quarter of 2026, with net sales down 6% year-over-year. The company noted that its Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure segment was particularly impacted by the ongoing Middle East conflict.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Affect Energy Markets
items.summary: The global natural gas market is expected to remain tight through 2027 due to ongoing Mideast conflict disrupting shipping and delaying LNG supply. Oil prices also climbed on Friday, driven by fears of prolonged supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Magnificent 7 Earnings Focus Shifts to AI Return on Investment
items.summary: As the remaining 'Magnificent 7' companies (Nvidia, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta) prepare to report earnings, investors are shifting their focus from pure AI and datacenter capital expenditures to the return on investment (ROI) from these significant investments.
